calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-13-2021 Off Calendar

Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Mar-09-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Jason M. Horst, Esq. of Horst Legal Counsel, 415.871.6567, appearing for the Plaintiff American Renaissance Corporation;
Atty. David Allen, Esq., Jaburg & Wilk P.C., 602.248.1000, appearing for Defendant Steven Shafarman.

Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Arbitration.

Matter called, ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff American Renaissance Corp.'s motion to compel arbitration is denied as moot. The Court has granted Defendants' motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

Motion To Dismiss; Declaration Of David L. Allen
Off Calendar Mar-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Jason M. Horst, Esq. of Horst Legal Counsel, 415.871.6567, appearing for the Plaintiff American Renaissance Corporation;
Atty. David Allen, Esq., Jaburg & Wilk P.C., 602.248.1000, appearing for Defendant Steven Shafarman.

DEFENDANTS STEVEN SHAFARMAN, SOMATIC AWARENESS RESEARCH AND EDUCATION AND FLEXAWARE LEARNING CORPORATION (FLEXAWARE)'s Motion To Dismiss.

Matter called, ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute is granted. This action was filed more than 11 years ago, on October 19, 2009. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 583.310, "[a]n action shall be brought to trial within five years after the action is commenced against the defendant." If a plaintiff does not comply with the five-year requirement, the action "shall be dismissed by the court or on its own motion or on motion of the defendant." (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 583.360(a).) In computing the five-year period, "there shall be excluded the time during which any of the following conditions existed: . . . Prosecution or trial of the action was stayed or enjoined." (Id. Sec. 583.340.) "Cases hold that a stay of proceedings pending the outcome of contractual arbitration tolls the five-year period under section 583.340(b)." (Gaines v. Fidelity National Title Ins. Co. (2016) 62 Cal.4th 1081, 1095.) "The party seeking resolution via contractual arbitration must also file a motion in the action at law to stay it (Secs. 1281.4, 1292.8); it will not be stayed automatically." (Brock v. Kaiser Foundation Hospitals (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1790, 1793.) Here, while the parties stipulated to binding arbitration on August 17, 2010, their stipulation did not contain any provision for a stay of the action, and the Court has never ordered such a stay. For that reason, cases in which there was a court-ordered stay are inapposite. (E.g., Brock, 10 Cal.App.4th at 1793, 1800 [holding that "an action at law stayed during the pendency of contractual arbitration proceedings" may not be dismissed for failure to bring it to trial within five years, following Byerly v. Sale (1988) 204 Cal.App.3d 1312 [same, noting that "[t]he lawsuit was stayed five months after it was filed, and the stay was continuously in effect until the trial court purported to dismiss the action under the mandatory five-year statute"]; see also Gaines, 62 Cal.4th at 1096 [Brock held that "a stay pending contractual arbitration tolled the five-year period under section 583.340(b)"].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
